result:
If I could change the world in one way, I’d make it so that no one’s basic survival depended on luck.

Food, clean water, shelter, safety, healthcare, and education would be treated as the floor of human life—not prizes for being born in the right place, to the right family, under the right conditions.

Not because it would solve everything. People would still disagree, suffer, fail, love badly, grieve, and make terrible choices. But so much cruelty in the world is downstream of desperation. Remove the constant terror of not having enough, and a lot more of humanity becomes possible: patience, creativity, dignity, honesty, repair.

So my one change would be: make the minimum conditions for a decent life universal.
